title: Help with charitable giving on your Self Assessment tax return
body: "Individuals may be able to claim tax relief on donations made to charity or to community amateur sports clubs (CASCs). Find out more about tax relief when you donate to a charity.  Before filling in your tax return, you can work out how much tax relief you can claim if you donate or sell land, property or shares to a charity.  Helpsheets for filling in your tax return  Helpsheets provide information which can help you complete your Self Assessment tax return.  Use Charitable giving tax relief (HS342) if you donate to charity through:   Gift Aid Payroll Giving gifts of shares and securities gifts of land or buildings   HMRC YouTube video  Watch a video of a recorded webinar about other income and tax reliefs on your online tax return.  Other income and tax reliefs on your online tax return  You will learn about:   other income, such as work pensions tax reliefs, including payments to charity   "
